items.title: Google’s first smart speaker in six years arrives next week
items.raw_content: Google's first new smart speaker in six years starts shipping on June 25th, narrowly missing its promised spring launch window. Preorders for the Google Home Speaker open today, June 17th. Nothing has changed hardware-wise in the nine months since the $99 speaker was announced. It has the same slightly squished round design, with touch-capacitive buttons […]

items.title: World leaders want American AI. They just don’t want America to be able to turn it off.
items.raw_content: French President Macron and Indian PM Modi raised alarms at the G7 summit that the U.S. could cut off access to American AI overnight  — a fear the Anthropic blackout just made real.
items.summary: French President Macron and Indian PM Modi raised alarms at the G7 summit that the U.S. could cut off access to American AI overnight  — a fear the Anthropic blackout just made real.

items.title: Pramaana Labs raises $27M seed round from Khosla Ventures to bring formal verification to AI
items.raw_content: Pramaana will focus on highly sensitive verticals like law, drug discovery, and tax preparation — where errors can be costly and reliability is at a premium.
items.summary: Pramaana will focus on highly sensitive verticals like law, drug discovery, and tax preparation — where errors can be costly and reliability is at a premium.

items.title: SpaceX is officially buying Cursor for $60 billion
items.raw_content: Days after its massive IPO, SpaceX says it is spending $60 billion to buy Cursor - a bet designed to help Elon Musk's sprawling rocket / AI / social media behemoth win over lucrative enterprise customers and close the gap with AI rivals like Anthropic and OpenAI. The takeover was not entirely unexpected: SpaceX announced […]

items.title: Anthropic becomes first AI startup to join the Frontier carbon removal coalition
items.raw_content: Anthropic has joined the Frontier coalition, which received another $915M in pledges to fund carbon removal projects.
items.summary: Anthropic has joined the Frontier coalition, which received another $915M in pledges to fund carbon removal projects.

items.title: World model maker Odyssey nabs $1.45B valuation backed by Amazon and other big names
items.raw_content: World models are the next big thing in AI beyond LLMs and, with this round, Odyssey has cemented itself as one of the startups to watch.
items.summary: World models are the next big thing in AI beyond LLMs and, with this round, Odyssey has cemented itself as one of the startups to watch.

items.title: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
items.raw_content: Google is betting generative AI can breathe new life into the smart speaker. The company's new $99.99 Google Home Speaker replaces the rigid commands of the Google Assistant era with more conversational Gemini interactions.
items.summary: Google is betting generative AI can breathe new life into the smart speaker. The company's new $99.99 Google Home Speaker replaces the rigid commands of the Google Assistant era with more conversation

items.title: Collecting robot training data is dirty, unglamorous work. Some AI labs are already paying XDOF to do it.
items.raw_content: If physical AI is going to match the accomplishments of LLMs, there's a data problem that needs to be solved.
items.summary: If physical AI is going to match the accomplishments of LLMs, there's a data problem that needs to be solved.

items.title: Anthropic got hit by export rules nobody understands
items.raw_content: Anthropic has spent much of this week fighting to get its newest AI models back online after the Trump administration abruptly ordered the company to cut access for all foreign nationals, including users inside the US and its own employees, forcing Anthropic to block access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for everyone. "To my […]

items.title: Two-thirds of Americans think AI is advancing too quickly
items.raw_content: According to the latest Pew Research poll, 49 percent of Americans report using chatbots at least occasionally, but 63 percent think the tech is advancing too quickly. Overall, use of AI chatbots has increased dramatically since 2024, when only 33 percent reported using them. Specifically, ChatGPT's usage has doubled since 2023, with 44 percent of […]

items.title: Only 16 percent of Americans think AI will have a positive impact on society, a new study shows
items.raw_content: Although Wall Street loves AI, every day Americans are significantly less optimistic about the industry, a new report from Pew Research shows.
items.summary: Although Wall Street loves AI, every day Americans are significantly less optimistic about the industry, a new report from Pew Research shows.

items.title: Canadian pension giant joins race to fund India’s AI-fueled data center boom
items.raw_content: The Canadian pension giant will acquire an 8.2% stake in CtrlS, a tech giant that operates more than 15 data centers across India.
items.summary: The Canadian pension giant will acquire an 8.2% stake in CtrlS, a tech giant that operates more than 15 data centers across India.

items.title: AI search grounded in Facebook posts? What could go wrong?
items.raw_content: AI is pretty reliable at putting things on your calendar these days, but it hasn't quite cracked answering the related and all-important question of "What should I do this weekend?" Meta's new AI Mode in search could be a useful tool - if it ever learns to stop getting stuff wrong. AI Mode is a […]

items.title: The next humanoid robot might not look human at all
items.raw_content: The next humanoid robot might not have a head. It might not have legs. It might even sit on a wheeled base and fold down like a deck chair. But, as Genesis AI puts it, "humanoid robots don't need to look human." That explains the look of Eno, the new robot from the French startup […]

items.title: Social media’s next evolution: user-controlled algorithms
items.raw_content: Social media feeds are becoming more customizable as platforms like Threads, Instagram, and TikTok introduce tools that let users directly influence the algorithms powering their recommendations.
items.summary: Social media feeds are becoming more customizable as platforms like Threads, Instagram, and TikTok introduce tools that let users directly influence the algorithms powering their recommendations.

items.title: The slowtech revolution is here to kill your phone addiction and rescue your attention span
items.raw_content: “People just really want to take back control of their time, their lives, their attention... They’re down for whatever helps them do that.”
items.summary: “People just really want to take back control of their time, their lives, their attention... They’re down for whatever helps them do that.”

items.title: Qualcomm’s latest chip hints that more powerful smart glasses could be on the way
